title: Testing, Debugging, and Quality Evidence · Guided Program Studio
summary: Verify the command-line data tool across pure logic, filesystem boundaries, failures, and changed inputs. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of a tested data-report release candidate while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Use assertions for programmer invariants and tests rather than recoverable user validation.
Test one observable behavior with arrange-act-assert structure, deterministic data, and meaningful failure output.
Use fixtures and parametrized cases to expose meaningful variation without shared mutable test state.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
